message:
bench(substrates): criterion harness for rvdna/ruqu v0.0

Adds criterion benches for the load-bearing operations of the two
v0.0 BackendAdapter substrates (ADR-007 §6, ADR-008 §4) and writes
the captured numbers to docs/research/benchmarks/v0.0-substrates.md.

  rvdna-backend/benches/
    pull_vectors.rs   -- {64,256,1024} x dim {128,384}
    cache_priming.rs  -- cold vs hot search_one through RuLake
                          (~555x ratio at n=256 dim=384)

  ruqu-backend/benches/
    simulate.rs           -- state_vector::simulate, n_qubits {6,10,14}
                              QFT-like H + CX-ring x 4 layers
    execute_and_pull.rs   -- end-to-end execute() + pull_vectors()
                              same sizes; isolates BackendAdapter overhead

Headline numbers:
  rvdna pull_vectors n=1024 dim=384 -> 40.3 µs (35.9 GiB/s)
  rvdna cold/hot ratio              -> 555x (33.5 ms -> 60.8 µs)
  ruqu  simulate n=14 (4 layers)    -> 848 µs (2.15 Gelem/s)
  ruqu  execute  n=14 (4 layers)    -> 1.15 ms (108 MiB/s payload)
  ruqu  pull_vectors n=14           -> 322 µs (381 MiB/s)

No src/tests touched. Both crates remain standalone (no workspace
per ADR-001). Only new dev-dep is criterion 0.5.

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+# v0.0 substrates — criterion benchmarks
+
+ruvector-rulake-rvdna 0.0.1 (ADR-007 §6) and ruvector-rulake-ruqu
+0.0.1 (ADR-008 §4), measured on `RuLake` v2.2 from crates.io.
+
+## What we measured + why these sizes
+
+We exercise the load-bearing operation of each BackendAdapter
+implementation: `pull_vectors` for rvdna's RAM-resident T0 cache,
+and `state_vector::simulate` + `execute` + `pull_vectors` for ruqu.
+rvdna sweeps {64, 256, 1024} vectors at dim ∈ {128, 384} — dim=384
+matches all-MiniLM-L6-v2, our reference embedding-class footprint;
+1024 vectors is the largest gene-block we expect from a single
+.rvdna section in v0.1. ruqu sweeps n_qubits ∈ {6, 10, 14} on a
+"QFT-like" alternating-H + CX-ring shape with 4 layers (n=14 is the
+soft ceiling before the v0.0 RAM-only design starts to feel its
+bound at 16,384 amplitudes). We additionally measured rvdna's
+cold-vs-hot cache ratio through the full `RuLake::search_one`
+path to confirm the cache earns its keep on the BackendAdapter
+contract. Hardware: Linux 6.17 / cargo bench profile (release +
+LTO thin + codegen-units=1).
+
+## rvdna-backend (T0 hot tier)
+
+| op                        | size                | mean      | p99       | bytes/sec    |
+|---------------------------|---------------------|-----------|-----------|--------------|
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=64,   dim=128     | 988.8 ns  | 1.02 µs   | 30.87 GiB/s  |
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=256,  dim=128     | 6.43 µs   | 6.49 µs   | 18.97 GiB/s  |
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=1024, dim=128     | 26.45 µs  | 26.61 µs  | 18.47 GiB/s  |
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=64,   dim=384     | 2.61 µs   | 2.65 µs   | 35.09 GiB/s  |
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=256,  dim=384     | 9.72 µs   | 10.11 µs  | 37.75 GiB/s  |
+| `pull_vectors`            | n=1024, dim=384     | 40.26 µs  | 41.73 µs  | 35.86 GiB/s  |
+| `search_one` cold (miss)  | n=256, dim=384, k=5 | 33.46 ms  | 34.59 ms  | n/a          |
+| `search_one` hot (hit)    | n=256, dim=384, k=5 | 60.78 µs  | 62.25 µs  | n/a          |
+
+Cold/hot cache ratio at n=256 dim=384: **~555x** (33.84 ms → 61.0 µs).
+
+## ruqu-backend (StateVector v0.0)
+
+| op                | size            | mean       | p99        | bytes/sec       |
+|-------------------|-----------------|------------|------------|-----------------|
+| `simulate`        | n=6,  4 layers  | 1.37 µs    | 2.44 µs    | 2.15 Gelem/s    |
+| `simulate`        | n=10, 4 layers  | 39.06 µs   | 70.55 µs   | 1.94 Gelem/s    |
+| `simulate`        | n=14, 4 layers  | 847.90 µs  | 1.20 ms    | 2.15 Gelem/s    |
+| `execute`         | n=6,  4 layers  | 2.88 µs    | 3.01 µs    | 170.04 MiB/s    |
+| `execute`         | n=10, 4 layers  | 56.85 µs   | 57.39 µs   | 137.79 MiB/s    |
+| `execute`         | n=14, 4 layers  | 1.15 ms    | 1.18 ms    | 107.94 MiB/s    |
+| `pull_vectors`    | n=6  (dim=64)   | 1.50 µs    | 2.09 µs    | 310.02 MiB/s    |
+| `pull_vectors`    | n=10 (dim=1024) | 24.43 µs   | 29.18 µs   | 316.20 MiB/s    |
+| `pull_vectors`    | n=14 (dim=16k)  | 321.61 µs  | 440.93 µs  | 381.29 MiB/s    |
+
+`simulate` throughput is reported as amplitudes-touched-per-second
+(state-dim × gate count), since each gate sweeps the full state
+vector. `execute` and `pull_vectors` bytes/sec are the f32 [re,im]
+serialised payload size.
+
+## What the numbers tell us
+
+- The cache is the headline result: 555x cold/hot ratio confirms
+  ADR-007's "share cache via content-addressed dedup" claim is real
+  in v0.0 — a 33 ms backend-side `pull_vectors` collapses to a
+  61 µs cache lookup once primed. This is the trust-chain payoff
+  the design is built around.
+- `pull_vectors` is **memcpy-bound**, not adapter-bound: rvdna hits
+  ~36 GiB/s at dim=384, which is RAM bandwidth territory on this
+  host. The `Vec<Vec<f32>>` clone path costs almost nothing on top
+  of the underlying copy. Surprisingly, the n=64 dim=128 case shows
+  *higher* GiB/s than n=256 — that's the 988 ns floor being
+  dominated by per-call overhead (BTreeMap lookup + Vec::clone
+  setup) rather than the 32 KiB payload.
+- ruqu's serialisation overhead is the obvious first target. At
+  n=14, `execute` (1.15 ms) is `simulate` (848 µs) + ~302 µs of
+  BackendAdapter work — the f64→f32 [re,im] flatten and the
+  `Vec<Vec<f32>>` allocation account for ~26% of end-to-end cost.
+  `pull_vectors` adds another 322 µs because every cache miss
+  re-clones that nested vec. A flat `Vec<f32>` of length `2*dim`
+  with a `dim` field would erase both, since the cache only ever
+  reads it linearly.
+
+## v0.1 perf goals
+
+- ruqu: get `execute` at n=14 under **900 µs** (from 1.15 ms; ~22%
+  cut) by storing amplitudes as a flat `Vec<f32>` of length
+  `2*dim` instead of `Vec<Vec<f32>>`, and bring `pull_vectors` at
+  n=14 under **80 µs** (from 322 µs) by serving an `Arc`-shared
+  buffer instead of cloning per call.
+- rvdna: hold cold-path `search_one` under **5 ms** at n=1024 /
+  dim=384 (extrapolated from n=256 cold ≈ 33 ms today, so ~7x
+  improvement) by mmap-ing section 1 in v0.1 instead of holding
+  the `Vec<Vec<f32>>` in RAM, and keep the hot path under
+  **70 µs** (currently 61 µs — non-regression budget).
